questions.q: Chandrashekhar Azad was a leading member of which revolutionary organisation?
questions.explanation: Azad was a foremost leader of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA).

questions.q: By what name, meaning 'free', is Chandrashekhar best remembered?
questions.explanation: He adopted the name 'Azad', meaning free, declaring he would never be captured alive.

questions.q: In which city did Chandrashekhar Azad die in a gun battle?
questions.explanation: Azad died in a gun battle at Alfred Park in Allahabad in February 1931.

questions.q: At which park did Chandrashekhar Azad make his final stand?
questions.explanation: Azad was killed at Alfred Park in Allahabad, now renamed Chandrashekhar Azad Park.

questions.q: What vow defined Chandrashekhar Azad's resistance to the British?
questions.explanation: Azad vowed never to be taken alive by the British, a pledge he kept to the end.

questions.q: Azad first gained fame as a teenager during which Gandhi-led movement?
questions.explanation: As a youth, Azad was arrested during the Non-Cooperation Movement of the early 1920s.

questions.q: Chandrashekhar Azad died in which year?
questions.explanation: Azad was killed at Alfred Park in February 1931.

questions.q: Azad was a close revolutionary comrade of which famous freedom fighter?
questions.explanation: Azad and Bhagat Singh were the two central figures who reorganised the HSRA.

questions.q: Chandrashekhar Azad was born in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Azad was born in the village of Bhabhra in present-day Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: What weapon did Azad reportedly use to end his own life rather than surrender?
questions.explanation: Surrounded by police, Azad fought on and used his last bullet on himself.

questions.q: When first caught and caned as a boy, what name did the young Azad reportedly give the magistrate?
questions.explanation: He gave his name as 'Azad', his father's name as 'Swatantrata', and home as 'jail', earning him the title.

questions.q: What was Chandrashekhar Azad's birth surname?
questions.explanation: He was born Chandrashekhar Tiwari and later took the title Azad.

questions.q: Azad was earlier associated with which organisation that preceded the HSRA?
questions.explanation: Azad belonged to the Hindustan Republican Association, later reorganised as the HSRA in 1928.

questions.q: Azad took part in which 1925 train robbery to fund the revolutionaries?
questions.explanation: Azad was among those involved in the 1925 Kakori train action and evaded capture.

questions.q: Many associates of Azad were caught after Kakori, but he notably did what?
questions.explanation: While comrades like Bismil were caught and executed, Azad escaped and remained at large.

questions.q: Azad helped reorganise the revolutionary body into the HSRA at a 1928 meeting held in which city?
questions.explanation: The Hindustan Republican Association was reorganised as the HSRA at Feroz Shah Kotla in Delhi in 1928.

questions.q: Azad helped plan the avenging of which leader's death after a lathi charge in 1928?
questions.explanation: The HSRA, including Azad, sought to avenge Lala Lajpat Rai, who died after a lathi charge during the Simon Commission protest.

questions.q: Which role did Azad take within the HSRA after its reorganisation?
questions.explanation: Azad served as commander-in-chief of the HSRA, coordinating its revolutionary activities.

questions.q: The park where Azad died was located in Allahabad, on the banks of which river system?
questions.explanation: Allahabad (Prayagraj) lies at the confluence of the Ganga and Yamuna rivers.

questions.q: After independence, Alfred Park in Allahabad was renamed in Azad's honour as what?
questions.explanation: Alfred Park was renamed Chandrashekhar Azad Park to commemorate his sacrifice.

questions.q: Azad's favourite pistol, which he carried, has been preserved at which museum in Allahabad?
questions.explanation: Azad's Colt pistol is preserved and displayed at the Allahabad Museum.

questions.q: Azad was deeply involved in planning revolutionary actions in which region during the late 1920s?
options: North India, around the United Provinces and Punjab
questions.explanation: Azad operated mainly across the United Provinces and Punjab in the late 1920s.

questions.q: In which year was Chandrashekhar Azad born?
questions.explanation: Azad was born on 23 July 1906 in Bhabhra village.

questions.q: As a boy, Azad studied Sanskrit at a school in which holy city?
questions.explanation: Azad studied Sanskrit at the Kashi Vidyapeeth in Varanasi (Banaras) as a youth.

questions.q: On the exact day Azad died, with whom was he reportedly meeting at Alfred Park?
questions.explanation: Azad was meeting a fellow revolutionary at the park when police, reportedly tipped off, surrounded him.

questions.q: Azad was involved in the HSRA action against which British officer to avenge Lajpat Rai?
questions.explanation: The HSRA shot police officer John Saunders in Lahore in December 1928 in mistaken revenge for Lajpat Rai.

questions.q: According to popular accounts, on which date in 1931 did Azad die at Alfred Park?
questions.explanation: Azad died at Alfred Park on 27 February 1931; 23 March was the date Bhagat Singh was executed.

questions.q: Azad's birthplace Bhabhra lies in which district of Madhya Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Bhabhra is in the Alirajpur district, formerly part of the Jhabua region of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Azad reportedly turned to revolutionary activity after disillusionment with which event of 1922?
options: The calling off of Non-Cooperation after Chauri Chaura
questions.explanation: Gandhi's suspension of Non-Cooperation after Chauri Chaura pushed many youths, including Azad, toward armed revolution.

questions.q: Azad reportedly used the alias of which profession while living underground in Jhansi?
questions.explanation: While hiding in the Jhansi region, Azad lived under assumed identities, working at a garage and teaching local children.
